last_scan.verdict: WHITEFISH LOCAL IS DRY. Local golf = one tiny static ~8-item pool (brand terms return same regional feed, all logged). Local Apple = ZERO MacBooks. National ship exists but research says ship-flips mostly net-negative after fees. 3 items re-scored: 1 personal buy (Ben declined), 2 killed as net-losers.
last_scan.geographic_insight: Shark economics favor LOCAL (0% fees) but Whitefish is too thin to produce local deals. The lane unlocks at NYC move (~Jul 20) = dense local market. Until then: rare ship-only on genuinely underpriced personal-priority items.
last_scan.note: Local golf market fully mapped this pass. Not thrashing more brand terms (redundant). Watches/furniture skipped (P2 / pre-NYC).
last_scan.scheduled_run_1232: 12:32 cron (scout+inbox, NO sends). Golf-local 3d = noise + 1 youth LH set $100 (skip). Apple-local = 0 MacBooks. extract.js listing_url+thumbnail capture VERIFIED working live. Zero active shark negotiations → nothing in inbox to advance. No new stageable deals — dry, as expected pre-NYC.

deal_fields_schema: Buy-side: listing_url, thumbnail, conversation[{from,text,ts}]. Sell-side (RESELL-SPEC): list_price, resale_channel, listed_date, sold_price, realized_profit. P&L: buy_cost (paid), hours (time in flip). Scout captures listing_url+thumbnail; negotiation logs conversation + increments top-level messages_today (S4 cap 35/day, reset on new date); resell loop fills sell-side fields on bought->sold.

deals.title: Callaway Opus wedges (3)
deals.condition_risk: Confirm count=3 + lofts (54/58 hold value best), shaft/flex, groove wear. $195/3 = $65ea = 35% under used-market.
deals.draft_opener: Hi! Are the Callaway Opus wedges still available? How many are there, which lofts, and what shaft + flex? How are the grooves/faces? I'm local in Kalispell/Whitefish and can pick up with cash today. Thanks!
deals.notes: BEST board hit. PERSONAL buy (Ben's wedge want) — walk 0.85xR capped at ask. Clears personal bar (>=15% under comp). Open 15-20% under ask w/ cash+local reason; settle ~$175. Marginal as pure flip (35% margin), strong as personal.

deals.title: Scotty Cameron Phantom 5, 33"
deals.condition_risk: 33" short = ~7% of sold volume = slow. Fake risk. Shipped-flip walk = 0.33xR = $104 vs $270 ask.
deals.notes: KILLED by framework 7/13. Buy $270 -> sell ~$330 - $25 ship - $43 eBay fee = net -$8 (LOSS). Ask 2.6x above shipped walk. Not surfaced to Ben.

deals.notes: KILLED by framework 7/13. Buy $275 -> sell $300 - $45 GOAT fee - $12 ship = net -$32 (LOSS), before fake risk that could zero it. Not surfaced to Ben.
